assertions.title: Concurrent first requests for one user collapse onto one VM assignment

steps.action: Register test user via WebAuthn virtual authenticator
steps.expected: User registered with valid session cookies

steps.action: Make 5 concurrent first protected requests to /api/shell/bootstrap
steps.expected: Requests collapse onto single VM assignment for user
steps.observed: All 5 requests returned 200 OK with sandbox_id: sandbox-m1 (static placeholder)

steps.action: SSH to node-b and check vmctl ownership state
steps.expected: Single VM ownership record for test user
steps.observed: No ownership.json file exists; multiple VM creation attempts logged for same user

steps.action: Check vmctl logs for VM creation patterns
steps.expected: Single VM boot sequence; pendingWaiters mechanism prevents duplicates
steps.observed: 3+ distinct VM creation attempts for same user (vm-6e7afcf5757e7fc4efbd3400d0454325, vm-78e320f15c0e467c1d292186e7b04c59, vm-3a72a7410a9c4e75dc21973c2d1f7b6f)

steps.action: Verify VM state directory and data.img creation
steps.expected: VM directories exist with data.img files, VMs boot successfully
steps.observed: VM directories exist (epoch file present) but data.img creation failed: 'truncate data image ... no such file or directory'

evidence.network: POST /auth/register (WebAuthn ceremony), GET /api/shell/bootstrap (5 parallel requests, all returned 200 with sandbox_id: sandbox-m1)

assertions.issues: CONCURRENT REQUESTS DID NOT COLLAPSE: Multiple VM creation attempts were made for the same user instead of collapsing onto a single VM. The pendingWaiters mechanism failed to prevent duplicate VM creation. Additionally, the fix for data.img directory creation (commit 2e1a9a8) is not working - all VM boots fail with 'truncate data image: no such file or directory'. System falls back to static placeholder sandbox (sandbox-m1) rather than VM-backed routing.

frictions.description: vmctl attempts to create data.img before VM state directory exists, causing all VM boots to fail
frictions.resolution: None - fix commit 2e1a9a8 claimed to fix this but issue persists

frictions.description: System falls back to static placeholder sandbox when VM creation fails, masking VM routing issues
frictions.resolution: None - this fallback behavior prevents proper testing of VM routing

blockers.description: VM state directory creation race condition: vmctl creates data.img before parent directory exists, causing 'truncate data image: no such file or directory' error on every VM boot attempt
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Verified fix commit 2e1a9a8 is deployed but issue persists; no non-disruptive fix available

blockers.description: Multiple VM creation attempts for same user indicate pendingWaiters mechanism is not functioning correctly - concurrent requests should collapse onto single VM assignment
blockers.quickFixAttempted: None - requires code fix for synchronization mechanism

summary: FAILED: Tested VAL-VM-004 (Concurrent VM assignment collapse). Expected: concurrent first requests collapse onto one VM ownership record. Actual: 3+ distinct VM creation attempts for same user (vm-6e7afcf5757e7fc4efbd3400d0454325, vm-78e320f15c0e467c1d292186e7b04c59, vm-3a72a7410a9c4e75dc21973c2d1f7b6f), all failing with data.img directory error. Root cause verification: Fix commit 2e1a9a8 ('create VM state directory before data image') is NOT working - error persists. No ownership.json file exists. System falls back to static placeholder sandbox (sandbox-m1). Concurrent VM assignment collapse mechanism is NOT functioning.
